name: content_hash binds to detected content instead of verdict record
description: The hash points to the raw scanned payload, not to the verdict. This breaks the composition model: the attestation should prove the verdict exists, not fingerprint the content.
reason: content_hash must bind to the receipt (verdict record) at url, not to the scanned content. Binding to detected content exposes enforcement internals and creates a bypass map.
note: This fixture is structurally valid but semantically wrong. The hash matches the SCANNED CONTENT, not the VERDICT at the url. A validator checking only format would pass this; a validator checking hash-against-url would fail it.
validation_level: requires url fetch or receipt inspection (not detectable by format-only validator)

name: Verdict url returns non-JSON (HTML error page)
description: The url resolves but returns an HTML error page instead of a JSON verdict record.
reason: url must return application/json. An HTML response is not a valid verdict record.
validation_level: requires url fetch or receipt inspection (not detectable by format-only validator)

name: Verifier DID does not match receipt issuer
description: The verifier field says did:web:alice.example but the receipt at url was signed by did:web:bob.example. The attestation envelope claims a verifier that did not produce the receipt.
reason: verifier must identify the entity that produced the receipt. Mismatch between envelope verifier and receipt issuer is a binding violation.
validation_level: requires url fetch or receipt inspection (not detectable by format-only validator)

name: Empty verdict record (zero-byte response)
description: The url returns a 200 with zero bytes. No verdict exists.
reason: A zero-byte response is not a valid verdict record. content_hash of empty content is the SHA-256 null hash, which should be treated as suspicious.
note: sha256:e3b0c44... is the hash of empty input. A conformance suite SHOULD flag this as a degenerate case even though the hash technically matches.
validation_level: requires url fetch or receipt inspection (not detectable by format-only validator)
